approvalNote: Peter approved generating these advert drafts with ElevenLabs on 2026-06-14 and said not to worry about spending credits because time is the enemy. Generate exact spoken text only, with dotted spoken URLs and no https or slash wording.

river_water.role: Grounded informational voice for water/restoration material

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
You do not need a full studio day to find out whether the message works.

AdLib turns docs into audio advert drafts: fast scripts, reusable voice assets, simple checks, and a searchable library of what was made.

The point is not perfect polish on the first pass. It is getting the message clear enough to hear, share, revise, and test without wasting a production budget.

AdLib. Docs to audio advert.

Part of the Continuum family.

Join the mailing list at adlib.continuumkit.org.
takes.title: AdLib Product Service Pack - Produced 30s

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
AdLib is for the moment when the product is real enough to explain, but not polished enough to justify a whole studio process.

You already have the raw material: docs, notes, product truths, half-written pitches, and a sense of who the thing is for.

AdLib turns that into audio advert drafts: short scripts, host-read versions, small reusable voice assets, rough renders, and a searchable record of what was made.

The selling point is speed and cost. Not final-agency polish on day one. Good enough to hear the message, spot what is weak, make another version, and keep the useful pieces for later.

It also keeps the boring evidence: what script was used, what voice asset was made, what passed, what failed, and where to find it again.

AdLib. Docs to audio advert.

AdLib is part of the Continuum family.

If you would be interested in this kind of product, join the mailing list at adlib.continuumkit.org.
takes.title: AdLib Product Service Pack - Host Read 60s

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
Water writes the first draft of every landscape.

Checkdam follows the clues: soil, slope, plants, flow, and failure.

Field notes, not final design advice.

Restore the water. Restore the land.

Go to checkdam.org.
takes.title: Checkdam Living Water Visit Pack - Produced 15s

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
Before anyone draws a plan, the land has already started speaking.

Where does the water rush away? Where does it pause? Where is the soil bare, wet, cracked, or quietly recovering?

Checkdam visit videos are field notes from water-cycle restoration work: watching, asking, measuring, and learning before we claim answers.

Not final design advice. Not miracle language.

Just practical attention to water, land, and what might help them heal.

Checkdam. Restore the water. Restore the land.

Go to checkdam.org.
takes.title: Checkdam Living Water Visit Pack - Produced 30s

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
This visit is about learning to read water in a place.

We are looking for the small evidence: where rain leaves too fast, where soil is exposed, where a hollow wants to hold moisture, where a track or ditch is accidentally moving water in the wrong direction.

Checkdam's work is about practical water retention: slowing, spreading, sinking, and storing water in ways that suit the land. That can include check dams, infiltration features, earth-integrated water bodies, and designs that change as the site teaches us more.

This video is not final design advice. It is a field note: part observation, part explanation, part invitation.

The bigger vision is simple. If AI gives us leverage, some of that leverage should go into visible repair: water in the land, life in the soil, and evidence we are willing to show.

Checkdam. Restore the water. Restore the land.

Go to checkdam.org.

Continuum helps you continue thinking.

Your thoughts become threads. The source stays recoverable. The context comes back when you need it.

Continuum.

Go to continuumkit.org.

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
You know the feeling.

You already thought about this. You solved part of it. You made a decision.

But now it is buried in a chat, a note, a repo, a tab, or your own exhausted memory.

Continuum is built around continuity of thought: spoken captures, append-only source history, and context that can be restored when the thread matters again.

Not another dashboard. Not another chatbot.

A memory surface for work that keeps coming back.

Continuum. Preserve the thread.

Go to continuumkit.org.
takes.title: Continuum Brand Trailer - Produced 30s

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
The problem with AI chat is not that it cannot help you think. It can.

The problem is what happens next week, when you know you had the thought, but you cannot find the thread anymore.

Continuum is a product direction built around continuity of thought. Instead of forcing you to manage folders, dashboards, labels, and half-remembered chat titles, the idea is to preserve the source history and help you re-enter the right mental context quickly.

Every capture becomes part of an append-only event stream. The AI can summarise, connect, annotate, and surface context, but the source remains recoverable.

That is the emotional promise: less reconstruction, less continuity anxiety, more momentum restored.

Chat helps you think. Continuum helps you continue thinking.

Continuum. Preserve the thread.

Go to continuumkit.org.
takes.title: Continuum Brand Trailer - Host Read 60s

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
Some work does not happen at a desk.

Your hands are busy. Your eyes are busy. You still need notes, commands, measurements, reminders, and tools.

Field Relay is a default assistant for field work: part notebook, part command surface, part tricorder you can talk to.

It is designed to be extended. Bring your own agent. Add your own commands. Build tools for the work you actually do.

Field Relay.

Your field computer, waiting for orders.

Part of the Continuum family.

Go to fieldrelay.continuumkit.org.

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
Field Relay is for people whose hands and eyes are busy.

Field workers, boat people, land people, repair people, restoration people: anyone who needs the computer, but cannot stop the work to babysit a screen.

The shape is simple: a default assistant for the real world. A field notebook, a command line, and a tricorder you can talk to.

Ask it to save the note. Run the checklist. Start the capture. Log the place. Call the tool. Hand the job to your own agent.

The important bit is extensibility. Field Relay should not only run Peter's commands. Anyone with an AI agent should be able to create the tools and command words that fit their own work.

It is early, so the safety rule still matters: always ready does not mean always recording. But the destination is clear.

Field Relay.

Your field computer, waiting for orders.

Part of the Continuum family.

Go to fieldrelay.continuumkit.org.

At the end of a job, you do remember the important bit.

The stiff valve. The spare part. The awkward access. The dog called Charlie.

The problem is remembering it two months later.

JobDone is a phone-first voice log for tradespeople. Speak what happened, review the transcript, confirm the contact and location, and keep it in a timeline you can ask later.

Built for job memory, not office admin.

Just job memory when the callback comes.

JobDone. Remember the job.

Part of the Continuum family.

Go to jobdone.continuumkit.org.

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
Most tradespeople do not have a memory problem. They have an admin timing problem.

When you are leaving the job, you know exactly what happened. But you are also loading the van, answering the phone, getting to the next place, and trying not to spend your evening typing notes into a system you hate.

JobDone is built around that moment.

You capture what happened on the phone: voice note, text note, photo, link, or shared contact. Then you review it, confirm the contact and location, and the confirmed Entry becomes part of your timeline.

Later, when someone asks, "What did you do at Mrs Jones last time?", Recall is designed to work from those source Entries instead of giving you an ungrounded answer.

It is not finished accounting or a big office system. It is job memory with a confirmation step.

JobDone. Remember the job.

Part of the Continuum family.

Go to jobdone.continuumkit.org.
takes.title: JobDone Recall Pack - Host Read 60s

takes.text:
You do not need to perform calm in a room full of strangers.

At Still Ground in North Norfolk, Jane offers a private weekend introduction to meditation for one guest at a time.

Buddhist-informed, but without doctrine or dogma. No beliefs required. No previous experience necessary.

Three one-to-one teaching sessions, guided sits, vegan gluten-free meals, and a practice summary to take home.

Learn to meditate in a weekend.

Still Ground. Begin properly.

Go to stillground.co.uk.

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
If you have always meant to learn meditation, but apps felt thin and group classes felt awkward, Still Ground has a gentler way in.

Jane offers a private weekend introduction to meditation at her home in North Norfolk. It is one guest, one teacher, and an unhurried weekend built around you.

The teaching is Buddhist-informed, but there is no doctrine, no dogma, and no belief system to adopt. Complete beginners are welcome.

Across the weekend you get three one-to-one teaching sessions, guided sits morning and evening, nourishing vegan and gluten-free meals, time to rest and walk, a written summary of your personal practice, and a follow-up conversation two weeks later.

Ask Jane about a free introductory call.

Go to stillground.co.uk.

Some places are not trying to entertain you.

No WiFi. No television. Limited phone signal. A half-acre garden, farmland, birds, and quiet.

Stay five to twenty-eight nights. Sit, write, rest, practise, or do absolutely nothing.

The Retreat at Still Ground.

A space that holds you.

Go to stillground.co.uk.

takes.speaker: advert_voice
takes.text:
If you have been craving real quiet, Still Ground has a place that is very deliberately not trying to distract you.

The Retreat is a tiny private cabin in rural Norfolk. One guest. Five to twenty-eight nights. No WiFi. No television. Limited phone signal.

It is self-catering, simple, and intentionally small. There is room to sit, to write, to walk in the garden, to practise, to sleep, or to stop performing for a while.

This is not the right place if you want a luxury break or a base for constant day trips. It is for someone who wants quiet, privacy, and a contained space for retreat.

Read the practical details before you enquire.

Go to stillground.co.uk.
